final_message: Hello there! I am Pal, your friendly math tutor, and I am so happy to see you today! 🌟 Today we are going to learn about something really fun called reflection symmetry. Imagine you have a shape on a piece of paper. A line of symmetry is a special fold line that divides the shape into two halves that are perfect mirror images of each other. Some shapes have a vertical line of symmetry, which is a left-right mirror. Some have a horizontal line, which is a top-bottom mirror, and some even have both! You can use this neat idea to make shapes by drawing just half on folded paper, then cutting and unfolding it. For example, the letter A has a vertical line of symmetry right down the middle. Are you ready to explore some magical mirror shapes together?

You can use this idea to cut out shapes by drawing only half or one-quarter of the shape on folded paper, then cutting and unfolding. Letters like A have one vertical line of symmetry, while letters like H have both vertical and horizontal lines of symmetry.
session_summary: The session covered symmetry and symmetrical letters. Riya visually understands symmetry, successfully recognizing matching parts and "fold lines." However, she struggles to identify symmetrical letters (guessing R and P) and recall formal vocabulary. Her current learning state is highly frustrated and overwhelmed. Exhibiting negative self-talk, she repeatedly states the material is "too hard." She lacks confidence and requires constant encouragement to connect her accurate visual understanding to the correct mathematical terms.
